questions.question: Explain the difference between STIX and TAXII in threat intelligence sharing. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: STIX is the standardized data format/language used to represent threat intelligence (e.g., IOCs, TTPs).
TAXII is the application protocol used to transport/exchange STIX data between servers and clients.
Analogy: STIX is the 'language' spoken, TAXII is the 'phone line' used for the conversation.

questions.question: Discuss the challenges organizations face when engaging in threat intelligence sharing and propose best practices to overcome them. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Challenge: Lack of trust and fear of exposing sensitive data or vulnerabilities.
Best Practice: Use TLP labels and participate in closed groups/ISACs with legal agreements (NDAs).
Challenge: Data overload and low-quality intelligence leading to alert fatigue.
Best Practice: Share contextualized TTPs mapped to frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK, not just raw IOCs.
Challenge: Technical integration and lack of standardization.
Best Practice: Adopt automated sharing using standard formats (STIX) and protocols (TAXII).
Best Practice: Seek legal counsel and use sharing communities that understand sector-specific regulations.
